pass634_export_failure_cause: UTF-8 BOM in export_recv_import_wrappers.java caused GhidraScriptLoadException. Only ghidra_pass634_recv_wrappers.log produced, no CSV/pcode/decompile output.
pass635_fix_applied: Saved Java script without BOM (first bytes 2F 2F 20 = // ). Ghidra ran exit 0.
recv_caller_absence_reason: All 9 WS2_32 socket API references in Ghidra's reference database are DATA type (IAT slot pointers only). Zero code-level CALL/BRANCH references exist. The CALL instructions reside in packed/VM-protected .aion1 code (VA > 0x114E2000) which Ghidra cannot statically disassemble.
binary_iat_scan_result: Zero hits. IAT reference patterns not found in available packed PE binary. Confirms callers are in unpacked/packed region.
static_path_exhausted_reason: recv/WSARecv callers are in packed .aion1 code not recoverable by Ghidra static analysis. No static export corpus reaches Path B from any socket API. Ghidra xref DB confirms 0 code-level callers. Binary scan confirms 0 IAT ref hits. 111-edge callgraph has 0 recv-related predecessors.
future_s2c_oracle_rationale: The oracle route (known-plaintext XOR ciphertext to recover keystream at a specific S2C frame) bypasses all static blockers: no need for RDX/RSI/[RBP+0]/BL static values. The approach already succeeded for C2S (pass616, 11/11 KXSEQ). S2C oracle needs: (1) frame number of an S2C packet with known plaintext, (2) exact UTF-16LE plaintext bytes, (3) raw encrypted bytes from PCAP. Best candidate: SM_VERSION world-entry packet or MOTD string.
reason: pass634 Java export failed due to UTF-8 BOM. pass635 fixed the BOM and ran successfully (exit 0). Definitive result: all WS2_32 socket API xrefs are DATA-type only (IAT slots), with zero code-level callers visible. The recv callers reside in packed/VM-protected .aion1 code above 0x114E2000 which is inaccessible to Ghidra static analysis. Static path is exhausted. S2C initial key cannot be derived by static methods alone.
next_action: Use the future_s2c_oracle_scaffold (pass634 tool) with a known-plaintext S2C frame from the PCAP capture. Identify SM_VERSION or MOTD packet fields. XOR against ciphertext to recover keystream and derive effective BL at that offset. Roll the stream cipher forward/backward to decode all S2C traffic.
